name: Acquedotto Anio Novus Ponte S. Antonio
description: The Ponte S. Antonio carried the Anio Novus aqueduct.

name: Acquedotto dei Sette Bassi
description: A Roman aqueduct supplying the Villa dei Sette Bassi.

name: Alba Fucens Aqueduct
description: The Alba Fucens Aqueduct is a Roman aqueduct dating from the later first century BCE.

name: Albarracín-Cella Roman aqueduct
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 25 D5 unnamed aqueduct (Albarracín-Cella, NE S. Cristo)

name: Andelos aqueduct
description: Unlabeled on the Barrington Atlas, this aqueduct supplied ancient Andelos

name: Ansignan aqueduct
description: The remains of a Roman aqueduct bridge over the Agly River.

name: Aqua Alexandrina
description: The Aqua Alexandrina was an ancient Roman aqueduct that carried water 22.4 km from Pantano Borghese to the Baths of Alexander in the Campus Martius. It was in use from the third to the eighth century AD.

name: Aqua Alsietina
description: The Aqua Alsietina was an aqueduct built by Augustus that drew water from the lacus Alsietinus (Lago di Martignano) and conducted it to Rome.

name: Aqua Anio Novus
description: Aqua Anio Novus is an ancient aqueduct supplying the city of Rome. Begun by Caius in A.D. 38, it was completed and dedicated by Claudius in A.D. 52.

name: Aqua Anio Vetus
description: Aqua Anio Vetus, an aqueduct serving Rome and built originally during the third century BC. The Anio Vetus carried water to Rome from the Aniene near Vicovaro.

name: Aqua Antoniniana
description: The Aqua Antoniniana was a branch of the Aqua Marcia that carried water to the Baths of Caracalla.

name: Aqua Appia
description: The earliest of Rome's aqueducts, the Aqua Appia was constructed in 312 BC by the censor Appius Claudius Caecus. The aqueduct runs a course of 16.4 km, entering Rome at the Porta Maggiore. Frontinus estimated the daily volume at 75,537 cubic meters.

name: Aqua Augusta (Naples)
description: The Aqua Augusta (Naples), also known as the Acquedotto romano del Serino, is a complex aqueduct system supplying Roman-period sites in the Bay of Naples. The aqueduct system was built between ca. 33 and 12 B.C. It supplied a number of Campanian centers, including Pompeii, Herculaneum, and Puteoli.

name: Aqua Claudia
description: The Aqua Claudia was one of the four great aqueducts of the ancient city of Rome. It was begun by Caius in A.D. 38 and completed by Claudius in A.D. 52.

name: Aqua Crabra
description: An aqueduct supplying villas in the area of the city of Tusculum, mentioned by both Cicero and Frontinus.

name: Aqua Hadriana
description: The aqueduct referred to as the “Aqua Hadriana” served the imperial palace in Constantinople.

name: Aqua Iulia
description: A Roman aqueduct built by Marcus Agrippa in 33 BC and expanded by Augustus between 11 and 4 BC.

name: Aqua Marcia
description: The Aqua Marcia was the longest of Rome's 11 aqueducts.

name: Aqua Tepula
description: The Aqua Tepula was an aqueduct built in 126 BC by the censors G. Servilius Caepio and L. Cassius Longinus.

name: Aqua Traiana
description: The Aqua Traiana was an aqueduct built by Trajan and inaugurated in AD 109.

name: Aqua Virgo
description: The Aqua Virgo was one of the 11 aqueducts supplying the city of Rome and was completed by Marcus Agrippa in 19 BC. The aqueduct was later revitalized by Pope Adrian I in the eighth century.

name: Aqueduct (Venafrum)
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 44 F3 unnamed aqueduct (Venafrum)

name: Aqueduct at *Malliacum
description: Twentieth-century reports indicate remains of an aqueduct.

name: Aqueduct at Castellum Tingitanum
description: Its ancient name is unknown.

name: Aqueduct at Roman Ilium
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 56 C2 unnamed aqueduct (Ilium)

name: Aqueduct at Vicus Augustanus
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 43 B3 unnamed aqueduct (Vicus Augustanus)

name: Aqueduct at Vorgium
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 7 C3 unnamed aqueduct (Vorgium)

name: Aqueduct east of Djebel Zid
description: An aqueduct connected to areas of centuriation north of Segermes

name: Aqueduct of the Arcioni, Rocca di Papa
description: The remaining segment of a Roman aqueduct in the valley of the Arcioni stream, comune of Rocca di Papa (Italy).

name: Aqueduct of the Gier
description: A Roman aqueduct of the first century AD supplying Lugdunum.

name: Aqueduct south of Djebel Zid
description: An aqueduct connected to areas of centuriation north of Segermes

name: Aqueduct supplying Alb(i)um Intimilium
description: Ancient name, if any, unknown (BAtlas 16 E2 unnamed aqueduct at Alb(i)um Intimilium)

name: Aqueduct supplying Col. Iulia Equestris, at Divonne-les-Bains
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 18 D3 unnamed aqueduct (Col. Iulia Equestris, at Divonne-les-Bains)

name: Aqueduct supplying Tubusuctu from the northwest
description: A Roman aqueduct supplying the city of Tubusuctu. It delivered water from the area of modern Thala Itchouren to the El Arouia cistern complex on the northern side of the city. Laoues' 2015 thesis reconsiders the 19th and 20th century reports and hypotheses concerning the aqueduct's catchment, sources, and route in light of extensive on-site survey and ethnographic work with local communities.

name: Aqueduct west of Djebel Zid
description: An aqueduct connected to areas of centuriation north of Segermes

name: Aqueducts supplying Kourion
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 72 B3 unnamed aqueduct group (Kourion)

name: Aspendos aqueduct
description: A Roman aqueduct with an inverted siphon. It carried water some 19 km to Aspendos with sources at the Gökçepinar and the Pinarbas spring.

name: Barbegal
description: At Barbegal in southern France are the remains of a Roman watermill complex.

name: Briord Roman aqueduct
description: The Briord Roman aqueduct.

name: Caldaccoli aqueduct
description: A first century A.D. Roman aqueduct that carried water from the locality known as "Caldaccoli" to baths in Pisa.

name: Castellum aquae
description: A building in Pompeii serving as the distribution point of water originating from the Acquedotto Serino, Claudio or Augusteo. The structure is also referred to as Castellum Divisorium.

name: Catacomba di San Giovanni
description: Ancient catacombs in the city of Siracusa, built between AD 315 and 360. The main gallery re-purposes parts of an earlier aqueduct.

name: Cornalvo Roman Dam and Aqueduct
description: The Roman dam at Cornalvo is a gravity dam with associated reservoir and aqueduct. It dates to the first or second century AD. The earthen dam and its stone cladding remain in use. The site is a part of the Archaeological Ensemble of Mérida, a UNESCO World Heritage site.

name: Delikkemer aqueduct
description: An inverted siphon aqueduct at Patara.

name: El Habel tunnel (Saldae aqueduct)
description: An aqueduct tunnel, near Saldae, sometimes referred to as the Nonius Datus tunnel. It was a component of a Roman aqueduct that delivered water from the area of modern Toudja to Saldae (modern Bejaia in Algeria's Kabylia province). The tunnel's modern name derives from the nearby village of El Habel, which is located just north of Meccuc in the municipal district of Bejaia.

name: Jerwan Aqueduct
description: The Assyrian king Sennacherib had an aqueduct constructed between 703 and 690 B.C. near the ancient city of Girmua. The structure is part of the "Khinnis [Canal] System." The identification of the structure's builder is confirmed by means of several inscriptions.

name: La Lône arcade, Nimes aqueduct
description: A segment of the Nimes aqueduct.

name: Meyrargues aqueduct
description: Remains of a Roman aqueduct.

name: Nîmes aqueduct
description: Supplied Nemausus with water from the mid-first to sixth centuries AD.

name: Ntourmiza Corinth Aqueduct arches
description: Arches related to the Hadrianic aqueduct supplying water to Corinth are found on the slope of the Dourmiza hill, Gymno, Korinthia, Greece.

name: Olba Aqueduct
description: The Olba Aqueduct is located in Mersin Province of southern Turkey. It was commissioned during the reign of Septimius Severus and restored under Justinian I.

name: Oued Cherchira Roman Bridge
description: A bridge over the Oued Cherchira in Tunisia.

name: Perrotte Aqueduct tunnel, aqueduct of Nimes
description: The aqueduct tunnel known as the Tunnel de la Perrotte stretches for more than 60 meters.

name: Pondel
description: A Roman aqueduct dating to 3 B.C.

name: Pons Agrippae
description: A debated bridge upriver from the modern Ponte Sisto.

name: Pont de Bornègre
description: A Roman aqueduct bridge connected to the Roman aqueduct of Nîmes.

name: Pont de Valmale
description: The so-called Pont de Valmale is part of the Roman aqueduct system supplying Nîmes.

name: Pont del Diable (aqueduct)
description: A Roman aqueduct built to supply the city of Tarraco.

name: Pont du Gard
description: The Pont du Gard is an ancient Roman aqueduct bridge that crosses the Gardon River near the town of Vers-Pont-du-Gard in the south of France. It was enrolled as a UNESCO World Heritage Site in 1985.

name: Ponte Barucelli
description: A Roman aqueduct bridge located in the comune of Gallicano nel Lazio (Rome, Italy). It was once part of the Aqua Claudia.

name: Ponte Cardona
description: An aqueduct bridge near Nequinum dating ca. A.D. 24 - 33.

name: Ponte degli Arcinelli
description: The so-called "Ponte degli Arcinelli" is a Roman aqueduct bridge that was part of the Aqua Anio Novus.

name: Ponte della Mola di San Gregorio da Sassola
description: A Roman aqueduct bridge that was part of the Aqua Anio Vetus. The remains of the bridge preserve a series of 22 arches stretching approximately 155 meters.

name: Ponte delle Torri
description: A thirteenth-century aqueduct at Spoletium.

name: Ponte Lupo
description: Remains of a portion of the Aqua Marcia aqueduct located near Gallicano nel Lazio.

name: Ponte Pischero
description: The ruin called Ponte Pischero was once an aqueduct bridge that was part of the Aqua Anio Vetus aqueduct.

name: Ponte sul Fosso di Biserano
description: The Ponte sul Fosso di Biserano is a Roman aqueduct bridge of the Aqua Anio Vetus.

name: Ponte Taulella
description: The ruin called Ponte Taulella was once part of the Aqua Anio Vetus aqueduct.

name: Ravenna Aqueduct
description: Supplied Ravenna with water from the Bedesis fl.

name: Roman aqueduct
description: The Roman aqueduct of San Giovanni.

name: Roman aqueduct at Arretium
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 42 B2 unnamed aqueduct (Arretium)

name: Roman Aqueduct at Moria
description: A well-preserved section of the 26 km aqueduct that supplied Mytilene on the island of Lesbos.

name: Roman Aqueduct at Mytilene
description: A large aqueduct that supplied the city of Mytilene from the west. Some parts of it are preserved.

name: Roman aqueduct bridge at Loukou
description: An aqueduct bridge crossing a small ravine, built of brick and now encrusted with stalactites.

name: Roman aqueduct near Limonum
description: Remains of a Roman aqueduct.

name: Roman Aqueduct of Knossos
description: A roman contour-grade aqueduct, carrying water from the Fundana spring, south of Knossos, to an inverted siphon bridge at Spilia and thence to Knossos.

name: Roman aqueduct, Spello
description: An aqueduct carrying water to Spello from the hills below Collepino.

name: Roman spring sanctuary near Heckenmünster
description: An extensive complex of structures dating to the 1st - 3rd centuries of the Roman imperial period sited at a pair of springs known today as "Viktoriaquelle" and "Schwefelquelle," near modern Heckenmünster in German. This site, whose ancient name and tutelary deities are not known, was extensively excavated in the 1960s. It comprised religious and performative spaces, as well as baths and hostelry.

name: Roman villa and aqueduct at Ponte delle Sette Miglia
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 43 C2 no. 22 (villa and aqueduct at Ponte delle Sette Miglia)

name: Römersteine im Zahlbachtal
description: Site of the remains of the Roman aqueduct, Römersteine im Zahlbachtal, in the district of Mainz-Bretzenheim.

name: San Lázaro Aqueduct
description: The San Lázaro Aqueduct is located to the north of Emerita Augusta in the locality Rabo de Buey-San Lázaro, as noted by the Barrington Atlas Directory.

name: Sant'Egidio del Monte Albino aqueduct
description: A subterranean Roman aqueduct in the modern Italian province of Salerno.

name: Segovia aqueduct
description: Supplied Segovia with water from mountains to the southeast

name: Segusio aqueduct
description: A Roman aqueduct of Segusio.

name: Skopje aqueduct
description: A Roman aqueduct in Macedonia.

name: Strongylo Aqueduct bridge
description: Remains of a Roman aqueduct bridge dating ca. 125 CE that once supplied ancient Corinth.

name: Titus Tunnel
description: A Flavian period channel cut for the purpose of diverting the course of a river.

name: Torrecuevas aqueduct
description: One of five Roman-era aqueducts known from Sexi (modern Almuñécar, Spain). This one is represented by an extant aqueduct bridge located near the source of the Rio Verde, north of Sexi.

name: Tunnel of Eupalinos
description: A sixth century B.C. aqueduct on Samos, the Tunnel of Eupalinos is an amphistomon, meaning it was excavated simultaneously from each terminus. The tunnel measures 1,036 m in length.

name: Unnamed aqueduct
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 86 B2 unnamed aqueduct (Heraclea)

name: Unnamed aqueduct
description: An unnamed aqueduct supplying water to Tarentum in south Italy that flowed from springs near Satyrion (aqua nymphalis).

name: Unnamed aqueduct
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 20 D5 unnamed aqueduct (Burnum)

name: Unnamed aqueduct (at Cremna)
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 65 E3 unnamed aqueduct (at Cremna)

name: Unnamed aqueduct (at Gaba Philippi)
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 69 B4 unnamed aqueduct (Gaba Philippi)

name: Unnamed aqueduct (at Phasaelis)
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 70 G1 unnamed aqueduct (Phasaelis)

name: Unnamed aqueduct (Ateste)
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 40 B2 unnamed aqueduct (Ateste)

name: Unnamed aqueduct (Myra)
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 65 C5 unnamed aqueduct (Myra)

name: Unnamed aqueduct (Sabratha)
description: An unnamed aqueduct supplying Sabratha.

name: Unnamed aqueduct (Torre del Monumento)
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 43 C4 unnamed aqueduct (Torre del Monumento)

name: Unnamed aqueduct at Alba Pompeia
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 39 C4 unnamed aqueduct (Alba Pompeia)

name: Unnamed aqueduct at Angitiae Lucus
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 44 D2 unnamed aqueduct (Angitiae Lucus)

name: Unnamed aqueduct at Augusta Bagiennorum
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 39 B4 unnamed aqueduct (Augusta Bagiennorum)

name: Unnamed aqueduct at Baeterrae
description: An aqueduct supplying Baeterrae from the north.

name: Unnamed Aqueduct at Cillium
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 33 C2 unnamed aqueduct (at Cillium)

name: Unnamed aqueduct at Ephesus (Aqua Iulia?)
description: The longest and most recently-constructed Roman aqueduct in the province of Asia, measuring 36.5 km from its source in the hills south of Ephesus to the metropolis itself. The aqueduct was first commissioned by the emperor Hadrian in 122-126, and it portions of it remained in use into the medieval period. In modern times, it is known as the Değirmendere Aqueduct, after the nearby Kenchrios river (modern Değirmendere).

name: Unnamed aqueduct at Lyktos
description: A roman-era aqueduct supplying the Cretan city of Lyktos

name: Unnamed aqueduct at Prusias ad Hypium
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 86 B3 unnamed aqueduct (Prusias ad Hypium)

name: Unnamed aqueduct at Sagalassos
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 65 E2 unnamed aqueduct (at Sagalassos)

name: Unnamed aqueduct at Sagalassos
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 65 E2 unnamed aqueduct (at Sagalassos) (1)

name: Unnamed Aqueduct at Sexi/Saxetanum
description: One of five aqueducts known from Roman-era Sexi. This one is represented by an aqueduct bridge whose extant remains parallel the SO-02 between the Ctra. de Málaga - Velilla and the Carrera de la Concepcion in modern Almuñécar, Spain.

name: Unnamed aqueduct at Vicetia
description: An aqueduct supplying Vicetia (modern Vicenza in Italy)

name: Unnamed aqueduct near Amiternum
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 42 E4 unnamed aqueduct (near Amiternum)

name: Unnamed aqueduct supplying Augusta Emerita
description: The so-called "Acueducto de los Milagros" (miraculous aqueduct) that supplied Augusta Emerita from the Lago de Proserpina north of the city. A small portion of the impressive aqueduct bridge still stands in Mérida.

name: Unnamed aqueduct supplying Diana Veteranorum
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 34 E2 unnamed aqueduct (Diana Veteranorum)

name: Unnamed aqueduct supplying Hippo Regius
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 31 H3 unnamed aqueduct (Hippo Regius)

name: Unnamed aqueduct supplying Lambaesis
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 34 E2 unnamed aqueduct (Lambaesis)

name: Unnamed aqueduct supplying Neviodunum
description: This ancient aqueduct supplied Neviodunum from the south.

name: Unnamed aqueduct supplying Rusicade/Thapsus
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 31 F3 unnamed aqueduct (Rusicade and spring)

name: Unnamed aqueduct supplying Saldae
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 31 C3 unnamed aqueduct (Saldae)

name: Unnamed aqueduct supplying Thamugadi
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 34 E2 unnamed aqueduct (Thamugadi)

name: Unnamed aqueduct supplying Thuburbi Minus
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 32 E3 unnamed aqueduct (Thuburbi Minus)

name: Unnamed aqueduct supplying Thubursicum Numidarum
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 31 H4 unnamed aqueduct (Thubursicum Numidarum)

name: Unnamed aqueduct(s) at Dolaucothi, Wales
description: A group of Roman-era aqueducts (a "leat system" in regional parlance and the archaeological literature for the area), probably supplying the gold mining works at Dolaucothi.

description: According to PECS, the Roman aqueduct at Eporedia originates "at the Viona stream on the slopes of the Mombarone".
