description: A Coptic monastery founded ca. 385/390 located 80 km north of Asyut, near the village of Dashlout, Egypt.

name: Apa Apollo, Mon.
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 77 E3 Apa Apollo, Mon.

name: Apa Bane, Mon.
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 77 D1 Apa Bane, Mon.

name: Apa Djoore, Mon.
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 77 F3 Apa Djoore, Mon.

name: Apa Epiphanius, Mon.
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 80 B2 Apa Epiphanius, Mon.

name: Apa Ioannes, Mon.
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 77 E2 Apa Ioannes, Mon.

name: Apa Makrobios, Mon.
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 77 E3 Apa Makrobios, Mon.

name: Apa Palemon, Mon.
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 77 G4 Apa Palemon, Mon.

name: Apa Psoi, Mon.
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 77 F3 Apa Psoi, Mon.

name: Apa Psote, Mon./Amba Bisada
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 77 F4 Apa Psote, Mon./Amba Bisada

name: Apa Senouthou (monastery)
description: The "White Monastery", located near modern Tahta and Sohag in Egypt.

name: Apa Souroutos, Mon.
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 77 E3 Apa Souroutos, Mon.

name: Apollo Asgelatos (temple)
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 61 B5 Apollo Asgelatos, T.

name: Apollo, T.
description: Two early Christian basilicae were superimposed over the ancient sanctuary of Apollo Dalios.

name: Apostoli, Mon.
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 89 D2 Apostoli, Mon.

name: Aqaba Church
description: A pre-Constantinian Christian church located at ancient Aila in Jordan.

name: Arachnaion (mountain)
description: A mountain of the Argolid with a higher western peak (Profitis Ilias, 1199 masl) and a lower eastern peak (Mavrovouni or Arna, 1139 masl). Material from the Mycenaean, Archaic, and Classical periods has been found on the western peak and identified with altars of Zeus and Hera described by Pausanias. Aeschylus mentions its use as a signal station.

name: Archaic Temple at Ai Lia
description: Badly damaged foundations of the peristyle of a large Doric temple of the 6th or early 5th c. BCE, the center of which is now occupied by the modern chapel of Profitis Ilias on a rise NE of ancient Kyreneia. This temple was probably destroyed during the earthquake of 373 BCE and replaced by a smaller temple to the west, built from its rubble. A monumental great altar has also been excavated immediately east of the temple.

name: Archbishop's Chapel, Ravenna
description: A private, early Christian oratory in Ravenna, Italy, erected by Peter II as archbishop in A.D. 495.

name: Arian Baptistry
description: Baptistry built by Theodoric the Great in the late fifth or early sixth century AD

name: Asabaa
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 35 E2 Asabaa

name: Aula Palatina
description: The Aula Palatina is a Roman Imperial Basilica of the emperor Constantine (German: Konstantinbasilika) located at Trier, Germany. It was built ca. A.D. 310 as part of a Roman palace complex.

name: Ayios Filon
description: A ruined fifth-century CE basilica on the Karpass peninsula of Cyprus.

name: Baldersdorf
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 19 F3 Baldersdorf

name: Baptistery of Butrint
description: The Baptistery of Butrint is a round structure, with a diameter of 14.5 meters. It was built in the sixth century A.D. and was excavated in May 1928 by Luigi Maria Ugolini.

name: Baptistry of Neon
description: An octagonal brick structure erected by Bishop Ursus (late fourth or early fifth century AD) as part of a Basilica (destroyed in 1734). Bishop Neon completed the baptistry at the end of the fifth century AD.

name: Barlaamus
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 68 A2 Barlaamus

name: Basilica
description: An important civic building in Pompeii and an early example of the building type, built between ca. 120 and 78 B.C. (VIII, 1, 1).

name: Basilica Aemilia
description: A basilica located at the juncture of the Sacra Via and the Argiletum flanking the Forum Romanum that was initially built in 179 B.C.E.

name: Basilica and convent of S. Ippolito
description: An important paleochristian complex located in the area of Portus.

name: Basilica at Aspendos
description: The remains of a Roman Basilica at Aspendos.

name: Basilica at Saepinum
description: The Basilica at Saepinum is positioned at the intersection of the cardo and the decumanus.

name: Basilica Benedettina di S. Michele Arcangelo
description: An abbey church located in Capua on the western slopes of Monte Tifata.

name: Basílica Casa Herrera
description: The Casa Herrera Basilica is part of the UNESCO World Heritage Site "Archaeological Ensemble of Mérida". The paleochristian basilica was built in either the late fifth or early sixth century CE.

name: Basilica Constantini
description: The Basilica Constantini, also referred to as Basilica Nova ("New Basilica"), Basilica of Maxentius or Basilica of Maxentius and Constantine, is a colossal fourth-century basilica located along the Sacra Via in Rome. It is considered the largest ancient structure in the valley of the Forum Romanum and is typically deemed the final Roman civic basilica built in the ancient city.

name: Basilica Damasiana
description: The basilica and oratory of Damasianus, as well as the martyr tombs of Simplicius, Faustinus, Beatrice, and Ruinianus.

name: Basilica Damous el-Karita
description: A Christian basilica of the Byzantine period located in Carthage.

name: Basílica de Santa Eulalia
description: The Basílica de Santa Eulalia is a paleochristian site that is included as part of the UNESCO World Heritage Site "Archaeological Ensemble of Mérida". The early Christian features were superimposed upon previous, preexisting Roman features.

name: Basílica de Son Peretó
description: A Paleochristian site dating to the fifth century A.D.

name: Basilica di San Marco
description: The ruins of a Christian basilica located near the Via Ardeatina on the grounds of the Calixtian complex have been connected with the Basilica of Saint Mark. The site was discovered accidentally in 1991 and is associated with the Catacomb of Balbina.

name: Basilica di Sant'Agnese fuori le mura
description: Pope Honorius I commissions the Basilica of Saint Agnes in the seventh century, replacing the earlier Constantinian structure.

name: Basilica di Santa Balbina all'Aventino
description: The basilica of Santa Balbina is a paleo-Christian basilica in Rome, located on the "little Aventine".

name: Basilica di Santa Sinforosa
description: A minor Paleochristian basilica located in Guidonia Montecelio that was dedicated to the patron saint of Tivoli.

name: Basilica di Santo Stefano in via Latina
description: The now-ruined basilica of Santo Stefano in Via Latina was a fifth-century CE early Christian basilica. It is now located within the confines of the archaeological park of the Via Latina tombs.

name: Basilica Hilariana
description: A basilica erected on the Caelian Hill in Rome under the patronage of Manius Publicus Hilarus and connected to the cult of Cybele and Attis.

name: Basilica Julia
description: The Basilica Julia was built on the south side of the Forum Romanum in 46 B.C. on the site of the former Basilica Sempronia.

name: Basilica Manastirine
description: An important extra-mural Christian site at Solina. The site includes the basilica and the cemetery of Manastirine, associated with a Christian martyr.

name: Basilica Neptuni
description: A building in the Campus Martius that originally dates to ca. 25 BCE.

name: Basilica of Campanopetra
description: The ruins of a three-aisled basilica of the sixth century CE located at Salamis in Cyprus.

name: Basilica of Carsulae
description: The civic Basilica of Carsulae is located on the forum.

name: Basilica of Constantine (Ostia)
description: The presence of a basilica created with funds donated by Constantine I in region 5 of ancient Ostia was known from textual sources and then confirmed via geophysical prospection and, ultimately, excavation.

name: Basilica of Cresconius
description: The Basilica of Cresconius is a church with a nave-and-side-aisle plan located at Cuicul (Djémila), Numidia, Algeria.

name: Basilica of Hildeguns
description: A basilica dating to the time of the Vandals.

name: Basilica of Iomnium
description: The basilica of Iomnium (Tigzirt) likely dates to the fifth and sixth centuries CE.

name: Basilica of Lechaion
description: The basilica of Lechaion was dedicated to Leonides, the bishop of Athens. It is one of the most important early Byzantine monuments in the Peloponnese.

name: Basilica of Lucus Feroniae
description: The Basilica of Lucus Feroniae communicates with the forum and is linked to the apsidal space identified as the Augusteum.

name: Basilica of Saint Augustine in Campo Marzio
description: The Basilica of Saint Augustine of Hippo in Camp Martius is a titular minor basilica of the Roman Catholic church located in the Campus Martius of Rome. The basilica was conceived in 1286 and the current basilica was completed in 1483.

name: Basilica of Saint-Pierre-aux-Nonnains
description: A Christian basilica that incorporates the remains of a fourth-century AD Roman gymnasium.

name: Basilica of San Clemente al Laterano
description: The eleventh-century Basilica of San Clemente al Laterano is a Roman Catholic basilica dedicated to Clement I. It sits atop a three-tiered series of architectural remains stretching back to the Republican period. The earliest phase is represented by the foundations of a building destroyed by the fire of A.D. 64.

name: Basilica of Sant'Apollinare in Classe
description: A basilica begun in the sixth century AD by Bishop Ursicinus with financial support from the Greek banker Iulianus Argentarius.

name: Basilica of Santa Maria in Trastevere
description: The Basilica di Santa Maria in Trastevere is a titular minor basilica located in the Trastevere district of Rome, Italy.

name: Basilica of Santa Pudenziana
description: A palaeochristian church dedicated to St. Pudentiana located in Rome and, perhaps, the earliest site of Christian worship in the city. The church is located along the Via Urbana, which follows the course of the ancient vicus Patricius.

name: Basilica of Septimius Severus
description: The Basilica of Septimius Severus belongs to the Severan Forum at Lepcis Magna.

name: Basilica of St. Achillios
description: The Basilica of St. Achillios at Larissa is an early Byzantine basilica dedicated to the city's patron saint, St. Achillios. The site was discovered and excavated in 1978.

name: Basilica of St. Crispinus
description: The fourth-century Basilica of St. Crispinus is one of the largest known in Africa.

name: Basilica of St. Francis of Assisi
description: A papal basilica begun ca. AD 1228.

name: Basilica of St. John
description: The Basilica of St. John the Evangelist is located in Ephesus on the Ayasuluk Hill. It was built between 548 and 565 CE.

name: Basilica of St. Titus
description: A Christian basilica of the mid-sixth to seventh centuries.

name: Basilica of the Sacred Heart (Paray-le-Monial)
description: 12th-century church in Paray-le-Monial, France. This site was a popular pilgrimage site and is an important example of Cluniac architecture.

name: Basilica of Thyrsos
description: A fifth or sixth century AD (Early Christian) triple-aisled basilica including mosaic floors.

name: Basilica Opimia
description: A civil basilica built in the Forum Romanum ca. 121 BCE by Lucius Opimius. This basilica was likely razed in 7 BCE.

name: Basilica Porcia
description: The Basilica Porcia is considered the first named civil basilica in Rome. The elder Cato is responsible for its construction in 184 BCE. It seems to have been a small structure that took the place of several earlier private atria located between the Clivus Argentarius and the Curia Hostilia. The Basilica Porcia was destroyed by fire in 52 BCE.

name: Basilica Ulpia
description: The Basilica Ulpia is located in the Forum of Trajan at Rome and derives its name from the emperor Trajan's gentilician nomenclature. The basilica was considered a marvel of ancient Roman architecture by observers, including the historian Ammianus Marcellinus.

name: Bau/Pboou
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 77 G4 Bau/Pboou

name: Bell-Loc d’Aro
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 25 H4 Bell-Loc d’Aro

name: Belmonte
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 45 E3 Belmonte

name: Bertelles
description: A "fortified basilica" in Cyrenaica, probably dating to the 6th century CE and associated with nearby farmsteads and tombs, the latter dating to the Roman period, but reused in late antiquity.

name: Bethabara
description: Bethabara, in modern Jordan, is an ancient site connected with John the Baptist. Bethabara is also attested on the sixth century A.D. "Madaba Map".

name: Biclaro?
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 25 F4 Biclaro?

name: Binbirkilise
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 66 C2 Binbirkilise

name: Bir Abu Swera
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 76 D5 Bir Abu Swera

name: Bir Ftouha basilica
description: An Early Christian pilgrimage complex and baptistry at Carthage.

name: Bishop Genesius Church
description: The Bishop Genesius Church at Jerash (Jordan) may be the earliest Christian church on the site. The church was built in the early seventh century, reusing a preexisting Roman temple.

name: Bıçkıcı Kalesi
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 66 A4 Bıçkıcı Kalesi

name: Blatades Monastery
description: A fourteenth century monastery.

name: Bou Kisnaden
description: This site, discussed in scholarly literature in the 20th century, seems from satellite imagery to have been impinged or destroyed in the construction of the Rocade Tipaza/Autoroute Tipaza - Cherchel in the early years of the 21st century. It seems to have been a villa with a central courtyard and primary entrance facing east, with a row of rooms with arched openings overlooking the wadi to the north. Vats and presses, possibly for processing grapes for wine, were discovered. Anselmino hypothesized that another, unexcavated structure immediately to the east was a church.

name: Breviglieri
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 35 F2 Breviglieri

name: Britaniensis/Britonensis Ecclesia
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 24 D1 Britaniensis/Britonensis Ecclesia

name: Brittablo
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 24 H4 Brittablo

name: Cantobennum, Mon.
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 14 I3 Cantobennum, Mon.

name: Castelfusano Paleochristian basilica
description: Situated along the via Severiana are the remains of an anonymous Paleochristian basilica that was in use between the second and the seventh centuries AD.

name: Castellnou de Basella
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 25 G4 Castellnou de Basella

name: Castle Acre Priory
description: A priory founded in 1089 by William de Warenne and consecrated sometime between 1146 and 1148. Home of the first Cluniac order in England.

name: Castrum Caetani
description: A Medieval fortification that incorporates and repurposes the Roman mausoleum of Caecilia Metella.

name: Cathedral of St. James, Jerusalem
description: A 12th-century Armenian church in Jerusalem, the principal church of the Armenian Patriarchate of Jerusalem.

name: Cathedral of the Holy Cross at Aghtamar
description: Armenian church built on the island of Aghtamar by King Gagik I Artsruni between 915-921 CE.

name: Çatıören Church
description: Byzantine church remains at Çatıören in Rough Cilicia

name: Cattedrale di Santa Maria di Amiternum
description: The ruins of the cathedral of the fifth century are located in an area of the archaeological site of Amiternum that was occupied in the second and third centuries CE.

name: Chafagi Aamer
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 35 F3 Chafagi Aamer

name: Chariton
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 70 G2 Chariton

name: Chiesa di San Paolo di Peltuinum
description: The church of San Paolo at Peltuinum likely dates to the seventh or eighth century CE.

name: Chiesa di Sant'Ilario a Port' Aurea
description: A sixth-, seventh-, and eighth-century Christian church located near the Port'Aurea of Benevento was built during the Longobard period. The church was built atop Imperial remains and was an apsidal hall.

name: Chiesa di Santa Pudenziana, Narni
description: The church of Santa Pudenziana near Narni, Italy, dates from the ninth to eleventh centuries CE and reuses spoliated architectural elements from a Roman villa.

name: Chiesa Ipogea di San Nicola
description: A poorly understood paleochristian site located on the grounds of the Inviolata park in the comune of Guidonia-Montecelio in the greater metropolitan area of Rome. The cave site may originally have functioned as a mithraeum.

name: Christian Basilica, Ostia
description: The so-called Christian Basilica, located in Regio III of ancient Ostia, was discovered by Guido Calza in 1939. The structure likely dates to the fourth and fifth centuries CE. The interpretation of the structure and its remains has proven difficult, resulting in numerous theories that are often at odds with one another. One possibility is that the complex functioned as a xenodochium (a guest house for pilgrims).

name: Christian House Church
description: Church adapted from a private house building in Dura-Europos.

name: Church and early Christian necropolis at Vaste
description: An early Christian complex that included a cult building with several construction phases and a vast cemetery.

name: Church at Shams el-Din
description: The church, well visible in Google Earth, is in the SW sector of the archaeological area

name: Church at Trimithis
description: A structure at the city of Trimithis (Amheida) in the Dakhleh Oasis, identified as a church and dated to the 4th century CE.

name: Church in Sidi Khiribech
description: Ruins of a church likely built around the fifth century AD, on the summit of the low hill of Sidi Khiribech in Berenike. The character of the building and its position has suggested to some archaeologists a defensive as well as a religious purpose.

name: Church of Mren
description: 7th-century Armenian church in the Kars region of Turkey. This church is now ruined, but still is the second-largest domed basilica from the 7th-century Caucasus still extant.

name: Church of Panagia Chalkeon
description: An eleventh-century A.D. Byzantine church in Thessaloniki.

name: Church of Prophet Elijah
description: A fourteenth-century AD church in Thessaloniki.

name: Church of Saint Alexander Nevsky, Jerusalem
description: The nineteenth century Russian Orthodox church preserves within it remains of Constantine's original fourth century church of the Holy Sepulchre.

name: Church of Saint Catherine, Thessaloniki
description: A late Byzantine church in Thessalonica.

name: Church of Saint Demetrius
description: A seventh century AD church dedicated to the patron saint of Thessalonica; the present basilica stands on the site of a precursor with phases beginning in the fourth century AD.

name: Church of Saint Eusebius (Ronciglione)
description: A paleochristian sanctuary located along the Via Francigena.

name: Church of Saint Gregory of Tigran
description: Armenian church in Ani, in modern Eastern Turkey, completed in 1215.

name: Church of Saint Martin at Moissac
description: A Christian church built, at least in part, atop the ruins of a Gallo-Roman building that include a hypocaust.

name: Church of Saint Panteleimon
description: The Church of Saint Panteleimon (Thessaloniki) is a late Byzantine church.

name: Church of Saint Sophia
description: The Church of Saint Sophia, also referred to as the Old Bishopric, is an Eastern Orthodox church located in Nesebar, eastern Bulgaria. The church originally dates to the sixth century.

name: Church of San Primitivo
description: The so-called Church of San Primitivo in Monte Compatri. The church's bell tower was converted to a defensive tower around 1200.

name: Church of St. Etchmiadzin at Soradir
description: Sixth-century Armenian church in the town of Soradir (modern Yanal, Turkey).

name: Church of St. George
description: One of a group of eleven rock-hewn monolithic churches located in Lalibela, a city in the Amhara Region of Ethiopia. The church likely dates to the twelfth or thirteenth century.

name: Church of St. Gião
description: A Paleochristian sanctuary in Portugal.

name: Church of St. Nicholas Orphanos
description: A fourteenth-century A.D. church in Thessalonica.

name: Church of St. Peter
description: A twelfth-century AD Christian church was built atop the remains of the Roman temple of Apollo.

name: Church of the Acheiropoietos
description: A fifth century A.D. Byzantine church.

name: Church of the Annunziatella
description: The church of the Annunziatella is a Catholic church located in Rome's Ardeatino district. It is situated between the via Ardeatina and via di Grotta Perfetta. The church was established in the thirteenth century, as an inscription of Pope Honorius III attests.

name: Church of the Archangels at Sige
description: Church in the village of Sige (modern Kumyaka) on the southern shore of the Sea of Marmara. Constructed under Constantine VI in 780, the structure was restored twice, in 1448 and 1819. It remained in continuous use until it was ultimately abandoned in 1922.

name: Church of the Holy Apostles
description: A fourteenth century church at Thessalonica.

name: Church of the Holy Apostles at Constantinople
description: Construction of a church dedicated to the Twelve Apostles at Constantinople began in 330 CE under Constantine the Great, and was completed during the reign of his son Constantius II (331-367). A larger church was established on the same site in 550 by Justinian I. The church was demolished by Mehmed II in 1462, and the Fatih Mosque was constructed on its site.

name: Church of the Nativity
description: A church that many claim marks the birthplace of Jesus Christ is located in Bethlehem, now in the State of Palestine in the West Bank. The original church built on the site was commissioned by Constantine I and dedicated in May 339 CE. The church was destroyed by fire, likely in 529, and replaced by Justinian I.

name: Church of the Saviour, Thessaloniki
description: The Church of the Saviour, Thessaloniki, is a fourteenth-century Byzantine church.

name: Civil Basilica at Aphrodisias
description: The Civil Basilica at Aphrodisias was a three-aisled public hall measuring approximately 145 by 30 meters. It opened off the southwest corner of the South Agora. It dates to the late first century CE.

name: Corvey
description: The abbey was first founded in 815 by Charlemagne's cousins, Wala and Adelard.

name: Costa Bellene/Ballene (Capo Don?)
description: A Roman mansio on the via Iulia Augusta. An Early Christian complex (basilica, baptistery, cemetery) developed nearby and lasted until the Middle Ages.

name: Crypt and church of San Lussorio
description: The Romanesque church of San Lussorio dates to the early twelfth century.

name: Cyriacus, Mon.
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 89 C3 Cyriacus, Mon.

name: Daphni Monastery
description: The current Daphni Monastery in Attica dates to the eleventh century, yet its earlier Christian phases belong to the sixth century AD when a Christian site grew atop the abandoned and destroyed sanctuary of Apollo Daphnaios.

name: Deir Abou Makar
description: A Coptic Orthodox monastery located in the Wadi El Natrun northwest of Cairo.

name: Deir Abu Fam
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 77 D1 Deir Abu Fam

name: Deir Abu Harmina
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 77 F3 Deir Abu Harmina

name: Deir Abu Hennes
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 77 D1 Deir Abu Hennes

name: Deir Abu Masis
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 77 F4 Deir Abu Masis

name: Deir Am-Nasara
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 77 F3 Deir Am-Nasara

name: Deir Amba Baramos
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 74 C4 Deir Amba Baramos

name: Deir Amba Bishoi
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 74 C4 Deir Amba Bishoi

name: Deir Anba Bakhum
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 77 F3 Deir Anba Bakhum

name: Deir Anba Pschoi/Deir Anba Bishai
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 77 D1 Deir Anba Pschoi/Deir Anba Bishai

name: Deir Antush
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 76 D5 Deir Antush

name: Deir Dronkeh
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 77 E2 Deir Dronkeh

name: Deir el Qal'a
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 70 G1 Deir el Qal'a

name: Deir el-Adra
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 77 F3 Deir el-Adra

name: Deir el-Bakhit
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 80 B2 Deir el-Bakhit

name: Deir el-Dik
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 77 D1 Deir el-Dik

name: Deir el-Kosseir
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 77 D2 Deir el-Kosseir

name: Deir el-Malek
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 77 G4 Deir el-Malek

name: Deir el-Muttin
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 77 E2 Deir el-Muttin

name: Deir el-Qattal el-Bizanti
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 71 A3 Deir el-Qattal el-Bizanti

name: Deir el-Shuhada
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 77 F3 Deir el-Shuhada

name: Deir el-Sourian
description: A monastery located in the modern Wadi El Natrun in Egypt, likely founded in the 6th century CE. The ancient church and some of its early decorative program survive.

name: Deir Madoud
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 77 F3 Deir Madoud

name: Deir Magara
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 77 E2 Deir Magara

name: Deir Rumhan
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 76 D5 Deir Rumhan

name: Deir Sombat
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 77 D1 Deir Sombat

name: Deir Umm Arad
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 76 D5 Deir Umm Arad

name: Deir Umm Butme
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 76 D5 Deir Umm Butme

name: Deirouné
description: An ancient place, located in modern Syria, with remains of a monastic chapel. Inscribed as a UNESCO World Heritage Site in 2011 as part of the "Ancient Villages of Northern Syria."

name: Dermech basilica
description: A Christian basilica of Byzantine Carthage.

name: Doss Trento
description: Doss Trento is a small hill that lies along the course of the Adige River. A Paleochristian basilica was located on the hillside.

name: Dranda
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 87 G2 Dranda

name: ed-Deir
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 76 E4 ed-Deir

name: Église Saint-Vincent de Marimbault
description: A 12th-century CE church thought to have been built on the site of a Gallo-Roman villa. It is located in the modern French commune of Marimbault (Gironde).

name: El Bovalar
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 25 F4 El Bovalar

name: el-Arba'in
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 76 E4 el-Arba'in

name: el-Fra'iyeh
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 76 D5 el-Fra'iyeh

name: el-Khirbet
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 76 D5 el-Khirbet

name: el-Ma'yen
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 76 D5 el-Ma'yen

name: Epaone
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 17 D3 Epaone

name: Episcopal complex of Eio-el Tolmo de Minateda (Héllín, Albacete, Spain)
description: The episcopal complex of Eio at El Tolmo de Minateda dates to the terminal sixth century CE and was in use during the following two centuries. Its construction may have been directed by Visigothic officials in Toletum.

name: Erebe
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 77 E2 Erebe

name: Eremo di San Michele (Coli)
description: The cavern of San Michele was a hermit's dwelling place that is located near a cavern known as the "cave of San Colombano". This hermitage lies in a relatively solitary area of the valley of the Curiasca di San Michele stream, a tributary of the Trebbia river (Italian municipality of Coli, province of Piacenza).

name: Eremo di San Michele Arcangelo
description: A rock-cut Christian sanctuary located on Monte Altino in the Monti Aurunci that was begun ca. A.D. 830.

name: Etchmiadzin Cathedral
description: A fourth century A.D. church located in Ejmiatsin, Armenia. This is the central cathedral of the Mother See of Holy Etchmiadzin of the Armenian Apostolic Church and was enrolled as a UNESCO World Heritage Site in 2000.

name: Euphrasian Basilica
description: An Early Byzantine Christian basilica located in Poreč, Croatia.

name: Faras Cathedral
description: The cathedral in Faras, founded in 620 and rebuilt in the 8th and 10th centuries, respectively, was the seat of the Diocese of Faras. The site had well-preserved wall paintings from the 8th to 9th centuries. The cathedral was flooded by the creation of Lake Nasser in the 1960s.

name: Farfa Abbey
description: A Benedictine abbey located ca. 60 km from Rome.

name: Farsh el-Qasr
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 76 E4 Farsh el-Qasr

name: Farsh Habash
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 76 D5 Farsh Habash

name: Forum and basilica at Venta
description: The forum-basilica at Venta date to the first and second centuries A.D. and are of the so-called "principia" type. The basilica measures ca. 54.6 x 20.1 m and was of the Corinthian order.

name: Gallarus Oratory
description: An early Christian church in Ireland, originally built between the sixth and the ninth centuries.

name: Gallo-Roman Structure at Moissac
description: A Gallo-Roman structure including hypocaust and other remains of a bath, located beneath the later Church of Saint Martin in modern Moissac, France. The bath may have belonged to a villa of the fourth-fifth centuries CE.

name: Gandzasar
description: 13th century Armenian cathedral located near the village of Vank in the disputed region of Nagorno-Karabakh/Artsakh.

name: Gantiadi
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 87 F1 Gantiadi

name: Garizein (mountain)
description: One of two mountains in the immediate vicinity of the West Bank city of Nablus.

name: Gasr el-Gatres
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 38 B1 Gasr el-Gatres

name: Gasr Stablous
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 38 C1 no. 13 (Gasr Stablous)

name: Gastaldaga area and the Episcopal complex
description: A Longobard complex in Cividale del Friuli.

name: Gebel Tafnis
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 79 D4 Gebel Tafnis

name: Gebel Teir
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 79 D2 Gebel Teir

name: Gerasimos
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 70 H2 Gerasimos

name: Germeshin
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 79 D4 Germeshin

name: Geth Rabbi
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 76 E4 Geth Rabbi

name: Glak, Mon.
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 89 D2 Glak, Mon.

name: Golyamo Belovo
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 22 B6 Golyamo Belovo

name: Great Church of Hippo Regius
description: The Great Church of Hippo Regius is located in the Christian quarter of Hippo. The church is associated with Saint Augustine, who was bishop in Hippo from 395 until he died in 430 CE.

name: Hagia Sophia
description: A major paleochristian, then Byzantine, church in Istanbul; subsequently an Islamic mosque, and today a museum.

name: Hagia Sophia, Thessaloniki
description: A church to Saint Sophia dating originally from the third century A.D.

name: Hasanaliler Basilica
description: Hasanaliler Church is a 6th-century Byzantine church.

name: Hephaisteion
description: The temple of Hephaistos located on the northwest side of the Agora of Athens dates to the middle of the fifth century B.C.

name: Horeb Mons, Mon.
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 76 D4 Horeb Mons, Mon.

name: Horvat 'Erav
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 69 B3 Horvat 'Erav

name: Hosios Loukas
description: A tenth-century walled monastery in Boeotia, Greece.

name: Hyrkania/Kastellion
description: Hyrkania was an ancient fortress in the Judaean Dessert that was rebuilt as the Byzantine monastery of Kastellion (ca. A.D 492).

name: I Cappuccini
description: The church and convent "I Cappuccini" in Frascati was the site of a Roman villa.

name: Ilıca
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 62 D3 Ilıca

name: Imst
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 19 C2 Imst

name: Iohannes Urtius, Mon.
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 89 C3 Iohannes Urtius, Mon.

name: Ishkhani (monastery)
description: A church and monastery in Tao-Klarjeti, first constructed in the 7th century.

name: Isieiou Oros
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 80 B5/ inset Isieiou Oros

name: Isola del Tinetto
description: An island in the Ligurian Sea.

name: Itea
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 55 C4 Itea

name: Itos
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 77 F3 Itos

name: Jebel Serbal
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 76 D4 Jebel Serbal

name: Kabaköy
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 87 E4 Kabaköy

name: Kalabaka
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 55 B1 Kalabaka

name: Kastri Livadiou ancient settlement
description: An archaeological site located 4.5km west southwest of modern Dolichi in Greece's Larisa regional unit (Thessaly). Here, a proto-byzantine walled settlement with three basilicas was built partly with spolia from a temple of Herakles and the ancient city of Doliche. On this basis, the excavators have argued for identification of the archaeology with the attested name; however, the lack so far of in-situ evidence predating the 6th century CE has led other scholars to continue to identify the original Classical-Roman polis of Doliche with the archaeological site at Sarandoporo.

name: Khirbet el Quneitira
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 70 G3 Khirbet el Quneitira

name: Khirbet Jedora
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 69 B4 Khirbet Jedora

name: Kirche auf der Akropolis
description: A Byzantine church located within the fortified acropolis of Aperlae.

name: Knowlton Church and Earth Works
description: A cluster of Neolithic and Bronze Age earthworks with a twelfth-century Norman church built in the center.

name: Köşkerli
description: Modern name for ruins of a Byzantine church and surrounding village in eastern Rough Cilicia

name: Kosmas kai Damianos, Mon.
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 53 A2 Kosmas kai Damianos, Mon.

name: Koustilya
description: A late-antique rural church now lacking decoration located about 6km north of the modern village of Tozeur in Tunisia.

name: Kraneia?
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 54 D3 Kraneia?

name: Kraneion Early Christian Basilica
description: An Early Christian Basilica located near the grove of Kraneion that dates to the sixth century CE.

name: Kursi paleochristian basilica
description: An early Christian basilica was excavated at Kursi, near the Sea of Galilee in the Golan Heights.

name: La Temple de la Madeleine, Geneva
description: The oldest Christian church in the city of Geneva, the Temple de la Madeleine site has been in continuous use as a church since the 5th century. The current structure on the site dates to the 15th century.

name: Latomos Monastery
description: A late fifth century AD church, later associated with a small monastery in Thessaloniki, Greece.

name: Laubendorf
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 19 F3 Laubendorf

name: Limani Ozia
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 54 B3 Limani Ozia

name: Little Hagia Sophia
description: former Greek Eastern Orthodox church in Istanbul converted to a mosque

name: Locoteiacus
description: A Benedictine abbey that is located 7 km south of Poitiers and was founded by Martin from 361 to 371.

name: Loutra Hypatis
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 55 C3 Loutra Hypatis

name: Lower Church at Banganarti
description: Church dating to the 7th century CE in Nubia that was succeeded by St. Raphael Church II. The site is located in the modern Sudanese village of Banganarti.

name: Luxeuil Abbey
description: Located in France's Burgundy region, the Luxeuil Abbey was founded ca. 585–590 by the Irish missionary Saint Columbanus.

name: Lysi
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 72 D2 Lysi

name: Madonna del Parto
description: Madonna del Parto is a Christian church at Sutri, dating to the thirteenth and fourteenth centuries. The rock-cut sanctuary repurposes a Mithraeum dating to the first and second centuries.

name: Madonnna del Canneto
description: The sanctuary site of Madonnna del Canneto is located at Settefrati within the confines of the Abruzzo national park in Italy. It has a long history as a sacred site, beginning with sacred activities dedicated to Mefitis in the later first millennium BCE.

name: Magnetum?
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 24 C3 Magnetum?

name: Makaravank'
description: Medieval Armenian church and monastic complex dated to the 9th-13th centuries.

name: Mănăstirea Halmyris
description: An Early Christian basilica and martyrium at Halmyris.

name: Mar Abay
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 89 C3 Mar Abay

name: Mar Abrohom
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 89 D3 Mar Abrohom

name: Mar Awgin
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 89 D3 Mar Awgin

name: Mar Matta
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 89 F4 Mar Matta

name: Mar Melki
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 89 D3 Mar Melki

name: Mar Yaqub
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 67 H2 Mar Yaqub

name: Mar Yohannon
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 89 D3 Mar Yohannon

name: Marcianum
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 44 F4 Marcianum

name: Marcianus Fundus
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 44 G4 Marcianus Fundus

name: Marialba Paleochristian Basilica
description: Marialba Paleochristian Basilica dates to the fourth and fifth centuries.

name: Marimbault Roman Villa
description: The 12th-century Eglise Saint-Vincent de Marimbault was built atop a probable Gallo-Roman villa.

name: Martyrion at Aphrodisias
description: The Roman Tetrakionion at Aphrodisias has been identified as a Martyrion. Its date of construction is uncertain, ranging from as early as the late second century CE to as late as the 5th-6th century CE.

name: Martyrios
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 70 G2 Martyrios

name: Mausoleum of Diocletian
description: A mausoleum built to entomb Diocletian at his retirement palace that was constructed around 295 CE and is located in modern day Split, Croatia.

name: Mausoleum of Galla Placidia
description: An ancient funerary chapel, part of the Santa Croce complex in Ravenna, Italy, possibly built as an imperial mausoleum (ca. AD 417 - 425). The empress Galla Placidia (d. AD 450 ) is the presumed, likely patron of the building, though she is very unlikely to have been entombed within one of three sarcophagi found inside.

name: Mausoleum of Theodoric
description: A monument built in Ravenna, Italy, ca. AD 520 by Theodoric the Great as his future tomb. After Belisarius reconquered Ravenna in 540, the tomb was converted into the church of Sta. Maria della Rotonda.

name: Megiste Laura Saba
description: An Eastern Orthodox Christian monastery overlooking the Kidron Valley located between the Old City of Jerusalem and the Dead Sea.

name: Meroth
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 69 C3 Meroth

name: Messon
description: An extensive ancient sanctuary complex on the island of Lesbos, thought to have been dedicated to Zeus, Hera and Dionysos and dating back to at least the 7th century BCE. A Christian basilica was built on the site in the late fourth century CE.

name: Metanoia Mon.
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 53 B2 Metanoia Mon.

name: Molzbichl
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 19 F3 Molzbichl

name: Monastery of Saint John the Theologian
description: The Monastery of Saint John the Theologian (also known as the Monastery of Saint John the Divine) is a Greek Orthodox monastery dating to AD 1088 on the island of Patmos.

name: Monastery of Saint Simeon Stylites
description: The monastery of Saint Simeon Stylites, located ca. 30 km northwest of Aleppo, Syria, is one of the oldest surviving Byzantine churches and dates to the fifth century. The church is built on the site of the pillar of St. Simeon Stylites.

name: Montebuono
description: The remains of a villa were excavated near the church of San Pietro ad centum muros in the nineteenth century. Based on epigraphic evidence, the site is connected with M. Vipsanius Agrippa.

name: Morulls
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 25 F4 Morulls

name: Musaifna
description: Excavations at Tell Museifneh (Eski Mosul) in the 1980s revealed remains of a well-built stone building that was identified as a Christian basilica dating to the seventh century A.D. The apisdal basilica measured ca. 50 x 50 meters.

name: Mushabbak Basilica
description: The remains of a well-preserved Byzantine church (ca. 470) located five km west of the Monastery of St Simeon. The ruins lie near the town of Daret A'zzeh.

name: Mygdaleza basilica
description: The remains of an Early Christian basilica dating to the fifth and sixth centuries.

name: Narbek
description: A rock-cut church located about 5km west of the modern town of Omar Mukhtar in Libya.

name: Nea Moni
description: An eleventh-century monastery on Chios built by Constantine IX Monomachos. The site was listed as a UNESCO World Heritage Site in 1990.

name: Nebo Mons (church)
description: Site of a modern church and monastery atop Mount Nebo in modern Jordan on a site commemorated by earlier churches built on a site traditionally held to be the burial location of the prophet Moses. The first ancient church here is mentioned earliest in the itinerary of Egeria.

name: Neklone
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 75 D2 Neklone

name: Nemea Early Christian basilica
description: An Early Christian basilica located southeast of ancient Nemea.

name: New Basilica of Saint Apollinaris
description: A basilica in Ravenna, Italy, erected by Theodoric the Great in the first quarter of the sixth century AD.

name: Nike (Monument at Marathon)
description: A monument to the Battle of Marathon. Eugene Vanderpool describes the location of the monument as lying in the northeastern part of the plain of Marathon. A Christian chapel known as the Panagia Mesosporitissa marks the vicinity of this monument. In the area of the chapel are a well that reuses ancient marble and the ruins of a medieval tower that was built from re-used ancient blocks, including a massive Ionic column capital and two large column drums.

name: Northern Cluster
description: The Northern Cluster of rock-cut churches at Lalibela.

name: Nostra Segnora de Mesumundu
description: Nostra Segnora de Mesumundu ("Our Lady of Mesumundu") is located in the territory of Siligo, Sardinia, Italy. This archaeological site preserves remains of multiple architectural phases. A mid-Imperial (likely second-century CE) Roman complex, which included baths, was reused, in part, for the construction of a Christian church in the sixth century.

name: Oberlienz
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 19 E3 Oberlienz

name: Onia
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 14 G1 Onia

name: Orbaniacum
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 14 G1 Orbaniacum

name: Örendibi
description: Modern name for ruins of a Byzantine church and surrounding rural settlement in eastern Rough Cilicia

name: Ostia Synagogue
description: The ancient synagogue located in Ostia Antica is one of the oldest known synagogues in the world. The structure's earliest phase dates to the reign of Claudius and it continued in use until the fifth century.

name: Palaiomonastero
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 61 D2 Palaiomonastero

name: Paleochristian basilica
description: A Paleochristian basilica was discovered at the eighth mile of the Via Tiburtina in 2003. The subterranean basilica reused an earlier quarry and may be related to San Sinforosa. The site dates from the late fourth to the middle of the fifth century CE.

name: Paleochristian Basilica
description: The Paleochristian Basilica in the Villa Gordiani Park is located adjacent to the mausoleum and immediately post-dates the tomb.

name: Paleochristian basilica at Vravrona
description: An early Christian basilica located west of ancient Brauron in Attica.

name: paleochristian basilica of Lugdunum Convenarum
description: The paleochristian basilica of Saint-Bertrand-de-Comminges.

name: Paleochristian basilica, Illa del Rei
description: The Paleochristian basilica located on Illa del Rei dates to the sixth century.

name: Paleochristian Church, Lin
description: A paleochristian church located in Lin, Korçë County, Albania, with fourth- and fifth-century mosaics.

name: Pampligua
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 24 H2 Pampligua

name: Panagia Angeloktistos
description: The church of Panagia tis Angeloktistis or Panagia Angeloktisti is a Byzantine church located in the village of Kiti, Cyprus. Its origins lies in the fifth and sixth centuries. It was listed as a tentative UNESCO World Heritage site in 2015.

name: Panagia Ekatontapiliani
description: Panagia Ekatontapiliani or Panagia Katapoliani is an important Early Christian church complex in Parikia town, on the island of Paros, Greece, that was founded in 326 CE.

name: Panagia tis Kyras
description: A twelfth-century CE Greek Orthodox church located north of modern Livadia on Cyprus' Karpasia peninsula. Its apse had been adapted from part of an older building and contained a 7th-century mosaic of the Theotokos (i.e., Mary, mother of Jesus).

name: Pantheon
description: A temple in the Campus Martius of Rome that was first built by Marcus Agrippa (ca. 27-25 B.C.) and then re-built by the emperor Hadrian (after A.D. 126). In A.D. 609 the structure was consecrated as a Christian church, S. Maria ad Martyres.

name: Paphor, Mon.
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 77 E2 Paphor, Mon.

name: Parco delle Tombe di via Latina
description: A cluster of Roman Imperial tombs along the Via Latina excavated in the nineteenth century.

name: Patriarchal Basilica of Santa Maria Assunta of Aquileia
description: The Basilica di Santa Maria Assunta, Aquileia dates originally to the fourth century.

name: Patriasdorf
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 19 E3 Patriasdorf

name: Peroushtitsa
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 22 B6 Peroushtitsa

name: Petrera
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 72 D2 Petrera

name: Petrera
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 72 C3 Petrera

name: Pfaffenhofen
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 19 D2 Pfaffenhofen

name: Phlabianai
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 64 B3 Phlabianai

name: Pianabella
description: A large Christian basilica discovered in 1976 several hundred meters southeast of the Porta Laurentina of Ostia. The original phase dates to the end of the fourth century A.D.

name: Pieve di San Giovanni Battista a Sillano
description: A ruined church that was once part of the diocese of Volterra, San Giovanni Battista a Sillano is unique in Tuscany for having a Romanesque-Norman façade. The ruined edifice likely dates to the twelfth and thirteenth centuries although the parish exists as early as the mid-tenth century.

name: Pieve Socana
description: Pieve di Sant'Antonino a Socana, a Medieval parish church, is built atop the remains of an Etruscan sanctuary.

name: Pirdop
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 22 B6 Pirdop

name: Pompeiacum/Vernemetis
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 14 F4 Pompeiacum/Vernemetis

name: Porta Maggiore Basilica
description: The so-called underground basilica near Rome's Porta Maggiore is situated 12 meters below ground level. This vaulted space with a triple nave design is decorated with an elaborate stucco work program. The "basilica" was discovered accidentally in 1917. The site has been dated to the first century BCE and is likely linked to the activities of the neo-Pythagoreans.

name: Ptghnavank
description: Church building in the modern village of Ptghni, Kotayk province, Armenia, dated to the 6th-7th centuries CE.

name: Ptoou Nkalamon
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 75 D3 Ptoou Nkalamon

name: Qal’at Sem’an/Church of Saint Simeon Stylites
description: An ancient church, located in modern Syria. Inscribed as a UNESCO World Heritage Site in 2011 as part of the "Ancient Villages of Northern Syria."

name: Qalat el-Baben
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 80 B4 Qalat el-Baben

name: Qalb Lozé
description: An ancient place, located in modern Syria, with remains of an ancient basilica. Inscribed as a UNESCO World Heritage Site in 2011 as part of the "Ancient Villages of Northern Syria."

name: Qalbloze
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 67 D4 Qalbloze

name: Qasr Libya, West Church
description: At Qasr Libya, the ancient Theodorias, two churhes have been identified. The Byzantine settlement was refounded under Justinian in 539 CE.

name: Qasr Serij
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 89 E4 Qasr Serij

name: Qenneshre
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 67 G3 Qenneshre

name: Ras Abu el Daraq
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 76 B3 Ras Abu el Daraq

name: Red Basilica
description: A Roman temple built above the Selinus river was originally constructed to worship Egyptian gods, most likely Isis and/or Serapis. In the Byzantine era, it was converted into a church of St. John. It was subsequently ruined, although the rotunda on the north side is still an active mosque.

name: Rephidim
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 76 D4 Rephidim

name: Riva S. Vitale
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 39 D2 Riva S. Vitale

name: Roman forum of Iulium Carnicum
description: The Roman forum and civic basilica at Iulium Carnicum (modern Zuglio)

name: S. Agapito
description: S. Agapito, the Palestrina Cathedral also known as the Cattedrale di Sant'Agapito martire, was built atop the ruins of Praeneste's civic forum and its temple. The relics of Saint Agapitus were transferred to this location in 898. The present basilica was consecrated on 16 December 1117 by Pope Pasquale I.

name: S. Agnese
description: A titular church on the Via Nomentana, S. Agnese houses the remains of the eponymous saint. The present church was built by Pope Honorius I in the seventh century AD, on the site of a fourth century basilica built by Constantine I.

name: S. Alessandro
description: The basilica and catacombs of S. Alessandro are located at the seventh milestone of the Via Nomentana.

name: S. Autonomos, Mon.
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 52 F3 S. Autonomos, Mon.

name: S. Benedictus
description: A monastery originally established by St. Benedict of Nursia in A.D. 529/530.

name: S. Clemens
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 43 E2 S. Clemens

name: S. Cornelia, Mon.
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 44 B1 S. Cornelia, Mon.

name: S. Cugat
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 25 H4 S. Cugat

name: S. Desideratus, Mon.
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 18 B3 S. Desideratus, Mon.

name: S. Laurentius
description: The Papal Basilica of Saint Lawrence outside the Walls (Basilica Papale di San Lorenzo fuori le Mura) is one of the Seven Pilgrim Churches of Rome. The basilica serves as the shrine and tomb dedicated to Saint Lawrence and its earliest phases likely belong to the fourth century AD.

name: S. Maria d’Artès
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 25 G4 S. Maria d’Artès

name: S. Maria di Galeria
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 43 B1 S. Maria di Galeria

name: S. Maria di Minerbe
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 39 H2 S. Maria di Minerbe

name: S. Martinus
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 44 E3 S. Martinus

name: S. Passera
description: S. Passera is a Christian church on the Tiber river in Rome, opposite the Basilica of Saint Paul Outside the Walls. The current church dates to the ninth century and incorporated the remains of a Roman tomb. The church is traditionally connected to a community of local miners.

name: S. Paulus
description: One of Rome's four major papal basilicae, S. Paulus was founded by Constantine I in the early fourth century A.D. and expanded by Valentinian I in the 370s. The basilica, with many renovations, repairs, and improvements, stood until the 19th century, when it was destroyed in a fire. The reconstructed basilica only generally resembles the original structure.

name: S. Petrus
description: The Basilica of St. Peter in Vatican City is currently a Renaissance edifice standing on the site of Old St. Peter's Basilica, a church built by Constantine I between AD 319 and 333. The holy site is located on the Mons Vaticanus in connection with the purported site of the burial of the Christian Saint Peter.

name: S. Quiricus, Mon.
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 14 I3 S. Quiricus, Mon.

name: S. Scholastica
description: A convent founded within the area of the Villa Neronis.

name: S. Sebastianus
description: S. Sebastianus (San Sebastiano fuori le mura) is a Christian basilica outside the walls of Rome; the earliest phase belongs to the fourth century AD.

name: S. Sinforosa
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 43 C2 S. Sinforosa

name: S. Thekla, Mon.
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 66 D4 S. Thekla, Mon.

name: S. Theodora
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 53 A2 S. Theodora

name: S. Victoriano
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 25 F3 S. Victoriano

name: S. Zion, Mon./Pharroa
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 65 C5 S. Zion, Mon./Pharroa

name: Sabiona
description: A seventeenth century Benedictine nunnery located on a site that shows occupational evidence stretching back to the Neolithic period and that includes substantial Roman and Late Antique phases.

name: Sacro Speco
description: The Monastery of Saint Benedict or Monastero di San Benedetto Sacro Speco. The Shrine of the Sacred Cave of St. Benedict (Sacro Speco di San Benedetto) at Subiaco is a significant monastic shrine connected to the Roman Catholic saint Benedict. At the site is found a fresco-adorned cave linked to the saint, dating to the eighth century.

name: Saint Gayane Church
description: A seventh century AD church located in Vagharshapat (Etchmiadzin), Armenia and built by Catholicos Ezra I. The church was listed as a UNESCO World Heritage Site in 2000.

name: Saint Hripsime Church
description: A fourth-century AD church was rebuilt in the seventh-century AD to house the remains of the eponymous saint. The church is located in the city of Vagharshapat (Etchmiadzin), in the Armavir Province of Armenia. Listed as a UNESCO World Heritage Site in 2000.

name: San Crisogono
description: San Crisogono (sacred to San Crisogono) is one of Rome's early parish churches (tituli). Pope Sylvester I (314–335) likely builds the first iteration in the fourth century AD; later reworkings occur in the twelfth and seventeenth centuries, respectively.

name: San Giacomo di Rialto
description: A church in Venice, Italy. San Giacomo di Rialto is claimed to be the oldest church in the city, with AD 421 as the traditional date of its consecration. The first documented attestations of the church belong to AD 1152.

name: San Giorgio in Velabro
description: A church dedicated to St. George located in Rome's Velabrum. Legend connects the church's location with the spot where the Roman she wolf found the abandoned twins, Romulus and Remus.

name: San Giovanni de Butris
description: A church of the eleventh and twelfth century built along the line of the Roman Via Flaminia. The church incorporates Roman remains, including those of a bridge.

name: San Giovanni in Oleo
description: A sixteenth-century chapel near the Porta Latina that is believed to commemorate the location where the Christian apostle John was reportedly unharmed after being immersed in a vat of boiling oil in 92 CE.

name: San Marcello al Corso
description: The church of San Marcello al Corso is located adjacent to the Via del Corso (the ancient Via Lata) in Rome. Originally known as the Titulus Marcelli, it was established by Pope Marcellus I before 418 CE. The current edifice was completed in the seventeenth century following a destruction by fire of the earlier church in 1519.

name: San Martino ai Monti
description: San Martino ai Monti is a minor basilica in Rome. The basilica was founded on a site donated by Equitius during the reign of Pope St. Sylvester I in the fourth century.

name: San Menna
description: The church of San Menna in Rome was recorded by the anonymous pilgrim author of the Einsiedeln in the eighth century CE.  It was located along the Via Ostiensis, most likely between the Porta San Paolo and the Basilica of Saint Paul. Likely founded in 589, the church is not attested after the tenth century.

name: San Nicola di Trullas
description: A Medieval church on the island of Sardinia that is dated to 1133.

name: San Nicola in Carcere
description: San Nicola in Carcere is a titular church in Rome's historical district (rione) Sant'Angelo, close to the Forum Boarium. The twelfth-century church is built atop the remains of Roman temples of the ancient Forum Holitorium and incorporates their remains into its structure.

name: San Pietro a Badia Pozzeveri
description: An abbey with tenth century origins connected to the pilgrimage route known as the Via Francigena.

name: San Pietro di Cantoni Italic sanctuary
description: An extra-urban Italic sanctuary located in the territory of ancient Saepinum.

name: San Saba, Rome
description: The Basilica of San Saba is located on the "Piccolo Aventino" in Rome. The basilica's origin is traced to 645 when fugitive monks from Mar Saba in Palestine traveled to Rome to attend the Lateran Council. They subsequently remained on the Aventine.

name: San Salvatore monastery
description: A monastery founded in A.D. 753 by Desiderius, future king of the Lombards. Located in modern Brescia, Italy.

name: San Teodoro al Palatino
description: A church on the Palatine Hill in Rome, sacred to Saint Theodore of Amasea, dating originally to the sixth century AD.

name: San Trifone in Posterula
description: San Trifone in Posterula is a no-longer-extant titular church of Rome. It was located at the corner of Via dei Portoghesi and Via della Scrofa in the Campus Martius. The church likely originates in the eighth century; it appears in documentary records beginning in 1006. The ancient church was destroyed in 1746 when the Basilica of Sant'Agostino was enlarged.

name: San Vitale
description: The basilica of Saints Vitalis, Valeris, Gervase, and Protase, commonly known as the Basilica di San Vitale, is a titular minor basilica church in Rome. The church was built ca. AD 400 by Pope Innocent I.

name: Sanctuary of Monte Sant'Angelo
description: A Catholic sanctuary on Mount Gargano, in northern Apulia, Italy. Listed as a UNESCO World Heritage Site in 2011.

name: Sanctuary of Poseidon at Tainaron
description: A famous ancient sanctuary of Poseidon located at the southern end of the modern Mani peninsula of the Peloponnese. Literary and archaeological evidence indicate that the sanctuary featured a temple, a cave treated as a mouth of the underworld, and other structural and monumental components. It seems to have served as a sanctuary site for suppliants and a context for the formal manumission of enslaved persons.

name: Sannabadae, Mon.
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 66 E2 Sannabadae, Mon.

name: Sant'Ilario
description: A paleochristian site located at the thirtieth mile of the ancient via Labicana, near to the ancient road station 'ad Bivium'.

name: Santa Cecilia in Trastevere
description: A church in Trastevere with origins in the fifth century CE. Pope Urban I likely founded the first church on the same site in the third century CE. Its venerated saint is a Christian martyr of the third century CE. Pope Paschal I rebuilt the church in 822 CE, and moved the relics of St Cecilia to the site from the Catacombs of San Callisto on the Via Appia.

name: Santa Costanza
description: A fourth century AD structure along the Via Nomentana that was built by Constantine I as a mausoleum, originally housing the remains of his daughters Constantina and Helena. It was dedicated as a Christian church in the Middle Ages.

name: Santa Maria Antiqua
description: A fifth-century A.D. Christian church built in the Forum Romanum at the foot of the Palatine Hill.

name: Santa Maria dell'Orto
description: The church of Santa Maria dell'Orto is a Roman Catholic church located in Rome's Trastevere district. Its foundation is traced to 508.

name: Santa Maria della Libera
description: An eleventh century church near Aquinum built largely from spolia.

name: Santa Maria delle Vigne
description: A Paleochristian church (fifth century CE) located near ancient Lavinium.

name: Santa Maria in Cosmedin
description: A minor basilica church in Rome, Italy. Tradition holds that the church was founded in the sixth century to serve the Greek community at Rome. The beginnings of the present edifice lie in the eighth century.

name: Santa Maria in Domnica
description: The Minor Basilica of St. Mary in Domnica alla Navicella, also known as Santa Maria in Domnica, is a Roman Catholic church in Rome, Italy. The basilica has ancient origins, with likely foundations in the fifth century A.D.

name: Santa Maria in Portuno
description: A vicus and villa of the Roman period that lay along a route connecting the Via Flaminia to the coast. Archaeological evidence for the production of ceramics and tiles has been identified. A Christian monastery is established in 1090.

name: Santa Maria in Tempulo
description: A deconsecrated Christian church on the flank of Rome's Caelian Hill, Santa Maria in Tempulo is connected with the ancient tradition of the spring and grove of Camenae.

name: Santa Sabina
description: A Christian basilica on the Aventine Hill in Rome was founded in the fourth century A.D. The church takes its name from a Christian martyr who was said to have lived on the Aventine. The church is the oldest extant Roman basilica structure that preserves its original plan in the city of Rome.

name: Santa Sofia
description: A Longobard church in Benevento, Italy, listed as a UNESCO World Heritage Site in 2011.

name: Santi Giovanni e Paolo
description: An ancient Christian basilica on the Caelian Hill in Rome that was built in AD 398 by will of a senator known as Pammachius. The basilica was located over the home of two Roman soldiers, John and Paul, who had been martyred under Julian in AD 362. The church was thus called the Titulus Pammachii.

name: Santi Quattro Coronati
description: Santi Quattro Coronati is an early Christian basilica on Rome's Caelian Hill begun by Pope Miltiades in the fourth century A.D.

name: Santuario dei Tre Santi (Alfio, Filadelfio e Cirino)
description: A church of ca. 1200 AD onwards, containing ancient material from the site of Apollonia in re-use.

name: Santuario di Maria Santissima del Lattani
description: A Marian sanctuary founded in 1430.

name: Santuario di Santa Maria di Barbana
description: The Santuario di Barbana is an ancient Marian shrine that dates ca. 582. It is said that Elia, the Patriarch of Aquileia, built a church near the hut of a hermit from Treviso by the name of Barbanus.

name: Santuario di Santa Maria Regina di Anglona
description: A Marian sanctuary located on the hill of Anglona in the province of Matera, Italy, that originates in the eleventh century.

name: Satıköy
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 64 B4 Satıköy

name: Seuera, Mon.
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 67 C4 Seuera, Mon.

name: Shyrvallos
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 72 A3 Shyrvallos

name: Sidi Said
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 38 C1 no. 11 (Sidi Said)

name: Sigillyah
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 76 D4 Sigillyah

name: Silla de Papa church
description: The remains of a church belonging to the early Medieval period (perhaps the seventh century) that perhaps was a project of Pymenius, Bishop of Asidonia. This edifice is located on the hill that was the site of the oppidum referred to as Silla de Papa.

name: Siniyah Island Monastery
description: A church and possible monastic complex identified in 2021/2022 on modern Siniyah Island in the emirate of Umm al-Quwain, UAE.

name: Siret el-Craat
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 38 D1 no. 26 (Siret el-Craat)

name: Siret el-Giamel/Gasrin di Beida
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 38 C1 no. 9 (Siret el-Giamel/Gasrin di Beida)

name: Siret er-Rcheim
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 38 C1 no. 12 (Siret er-Rcheim)

name: So-called Caesareum of Cyrene
description: A large architectural complex on the east side of the acropolis ridge in Cyrene that was probably built at the beginning of the first century AD and used as a gymnasium, but was later repurposed as forum in Flavian times, when the rooms north of the complex were levelled in order to build a large basilica. Various ruins can still be seen, including the podium of a temple in the central area, possibly dedicated to Dionysos. A fragmentary inscription referring to the "portico of Caesar", found over the inner side of the porch, made the place known as Caesareum.

name: So-called Central Church in Apollonia
description: A three-aisled basilical church, with an external apse, situated near the centre of the ancient settlement of Apollonia (Cyrenaica), about half way between the East and West churches. The building has a narthex, a small western atrium and a range of added rooms along the north flank. The mosaics have been dated around the sixth century AD, but the building seems to have been built in earlier times (possibly fifth century AD).

name: So-called Early Christian Basilica
description: A small basilica located outside the walls of the ancient Greek city of Akragas, at the foot of the so-called Temple of Juno, on the eastern slopes of the Hill of the Temples, in an area previously occupied by the necropolis of the late Roman period.

name: So-called East Church in Apollonia
description: A Christian church located in the ancient city of Apollonia (Cyrenaica) built approximately in the fifth century A.D., probably on earlier Hellenistic foundations. The site was excavated in 1922 and shows a three-aisled nave with transept at the eastern end, and a single apse. Traces of the mosaic floor are still visible.

name: So-called West Church in Apollonia
description: A large architectural complex that includes a three-aisled church with a projecting western apse, a small atrium, a baptistery, and other structures built up against the inner face of the western stretch of the city wall of Apollonia.

name: Son Bou Basilica
description: A three-aisled Paleochristian basilica of the fifth century A.D. located in the village of Son Bou in Menorca, Spain.

name: Sorna Roman Villa and Church
description: Near Parentium (today Porec/Parenzo in Croatia), Cape Sorna (Zorna) first hosted a large villa maritima, and then a small early Christian church.

name: Southeastern Cluster
description: The Southeastern Cluster of rock-cut churches at Lalibela.

name: Speos Artemidos
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 77 D1 Speos Artemidos

name: SS. Marcellino e Pietro
description: The church of Saints Marcellinus and Peter is a Roman Catholic church located on the Via Merulana in Rome. Pope Siricius built the original church in the fourth century A.D., close to the catacombs of Marcellinus and Peter. The relics of the saints were deposited in the church in 1256.

name: St Peters Church, Flawford
description: A ruined Medieval church located on the site of a Roman villa.

name: St-Estève-de-Ménerbes
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 15 E2 St-Estève-de-Ménerbes

name: St. Pierre Cathedral
description: Church in Geneva, Switzerland that was originally a Roman Catholic cathedral but was converted into a Protestant church during the Reformation. The original church was built in the 4th century but the present building began construction around 1150.

name: St. Raphael Church II (Banganarti)
description: Mid-11th to mid-18th century church built on top of an older church that was an important pilgrimage site in Nubia. The church is located in the modern Sudanese village of Banganarti.

name: Stancija Blek
description: The archaeological site of Stancija Blek (Tar-Vabrega, Istria-Croatia) is located on the west coast of Istria. In antiquity, the site would have been located in the territory of Parentium. Archaeological investigation has documented more than 1,500 years of occupation, beginning perhaps with a Roman-period agricultural establishment (villa) that was subsequently followed by Late Antique and Medieval phases that include a so-called tower. Nearby are the remains of the church of Santa Croce.

name: Ste-Cécile Paleochristian Church
description: Remains of this paleochristian church, located at modern Loupian in Hérault, France, were discovered and excavated in the 1980s. The structure lies immediately north of the current church of Ste-Cécile, which dates to the 14th century.

name: Suk el-Oti
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 35 G3 Suk el-Oti

name: Symeon, Mon.
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 67 C4 Symeon, Mon.

name: Tabennisis
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 80 A1 Tabennisis

name: Taberna Frigida
description: Taberna Frigida was a road station on the Via Aemilia Scauri at the crossing of the Frigidus river, located between Pisae and Luna.

name: Tagai
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 66 D4 Tagai

name: Tatev
description: A ninth-century monastery located on a large basalt plateau in southeastern Armenia.

name: Tebedut
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 35 E2 Tebedut

name: Temes
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 24 D2 Temes

name: Temple de Saint-Gervais
description: Church in Geneva, Switzerland that has existed in some form since the 4th century CE. The 4th century building was followed by a 10th century Romanesque church which was destroyed by fire in the 14th century. The current church was constructed in the 15th century.

name: Temple of Apollo Lepsios
description: A temple of Apollo on the Aegean island of Lepsia, generally thought to have been located in the vicinity of the modern church of Hagios Nikolaos. It is attested in various ancient sources, including a recently published epigraphic document demarcating the temple lands.

name: Thaur
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 19 D2 Thaur

name: Thekoa
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 69 B4 Thekoa

name: Theodosios
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 70 G2 Theodosios

name: Theoktistos
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 70 G2 Theoktistos

name: Theotokos Kosmosoteira
description: A twelfth-century monastic foundation and fortified church in the modern town of Feres in Greece's Evros prefecture. The structure served as a mosque during the Ottoman empire and was returned to use as a church in the 20th century.

name: Tholas
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 76 D4 Tholas

name: Thomas, Mon.
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 67 B4 Thomas, Mon.

name: Tigani basilica
description: An Early Christian basilica dating to the fifth and sixth centuries CE.

name: Titkois
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 77 D1 Titkois

name: Torre del Fum
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 25 I4 Torre del Fum

name: Tou Batou
description: A monastery built by order of Emperor Justinian I. St. Catherine's at the foot of Mount Sinai in modern Egypt.

name: Tsenti
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 80 B2 Tsenti

name: Tura d’ Urhay
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 67 H2 Tura d’ Urhay

name: Türkmen Baba
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 62 D3 Türkmen Baba

name: Unnamed monastery
description: A monastery founded in late antiquity on the island then known as Lerina/Planasia (modern Île Saint-Honorat, France; BAtlas 16 D2 unnamed monastery)

name: Untitled
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 38 D1 no. 21 (Ras el-Hilal)

name: Untitled
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 38 D1 no. 28 (Siret Bu Hosc)

name: Untitled
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 34 F2 unnamed feature ()

name: Untitled
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 34 F2 unnamed feature ()

description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 34 F2 unnamed feature ()

description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 34 F2 unnamed feature ()

description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 34 F2 unnamed feature ()

description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 34 F2 unnamed feature ()

description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 34 E2 unnamed feature ()

description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 34 E2 unnamed feature ()

description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 34 E2 unnamed feature ()

description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 34 E2 unnamed feature ()

description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 34 E2 unnamed feature ()

description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 34 E2 unnamed feature ()

description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 34 E2 unnamed feature ()
